observed_wrong_answer: a son
expected_answer: the son
diagnosis: Student missed the definite article ה.
remediation_hint: Review how prefixed ה changes a bare noun into a definite noun.

observed_wrong_answer: Student says the ה is part of the shoresh.
expected_answer: Student identifies ה as an added definite marker.
diagnosis: Student treated the definite article as if it belonged to the base noun.
remediation_hint: Compare the noun with and without prefixed ה to see what changed.

observed_wrong_answer: Student leaves out 'and'.
expected_answer: Student includes the conjunction meaning.
diagnosis: Student missed prefixed ו as a conjunction.
remediation_hint: Check whether the first letter is adding 'and' before the noun.

observed_wrong_answer: Student says the ו is part of the noun.
expected_answer: Student tests whether the ו is an added conjunction.
diagnosis: Student treated prefixed ו as part of the base word.
remediation_hint: Try removing the first ו and see whether a known noun remains.

observed_wrong_answer: Student translates a masculine plural as singular.
expected_answer: Student identifies a masculine plural noun.
diagnosis: Student missed the masculine plural ending ים.
remediation_hint: Review how many masculine plurals end in ים and re-check the noun ending.

observed_wrong_answer: Student translates a feminine plural as singular.
expected_answer: Student identifies a feminine plural noun.
diagnosis: Student missed the feminine plural ending ות.
remediation_hint: Review how many feminine plurals end in ות and re-check the noun ending.

observed_wrong_answer: Student leaves the noun as if the final ה stayed unchanged.
expected_answer: Student recognizes the ה-to-ת construct change.
diagnosis: Student missed the common ה-to-ת shift in סמיכות.
remediation_hint: Compare the construct form to the base noun and look for the changed final letter.

observed_wrong_answer: Student fails to connect a י construct form to a base noun ending in ים.
expected_answer: Student recognizes the ים-to-י change in construct form.
diagnosis: Student missed the common plural construct shortening from ים to י.
remediation_hint: Try restoring the fuller plural ending and then read the phrase again.

observed_wrong_answer: Student translates the two nouns as two unrelated nouns.
expected_answer: Student reads the noun pair as a phrase relationship or preserves ambiguity.
diagnosis: Student failed to test whether the noun pair forms סמיכות or another context-driven unit.
remediation_hint: Read the nouns together and ask whether the first noun is connected to the second by an implied relationship.

observed_wrong_answer: Student translates every את as 'with'.
expected_answer: Student distinguishes object-marker and with-sense uses.
diagnosis: Student overgeneralized the companionship meaning of את.
remediation_hint: Check whether את is really adding 'with' or only marking the direct object.

observed_wrong_answer: Student translates את as a separate English word in every object-marker use.
expected_answer: Student leaves object-marker את untranslated when appropriate.
diagnosis: Student did not recognize את as a direct-object marker.
remediation_hint: Use את to identify the noun receiving the action rather than forcing an English gloss every time.

observed_wrong_answer: Student ignores the suffix attached to את.
expected_answer: Student reads the suffix and identifies the referenced person.
diagnosis: Student missed the pronominal suffix attached to an את-form.
remediation_hint: Look at the ending attached to את before translating the form.

observed_wrong_answer: Student confuses אותו and אותה.
expected_answer: Student distinguishes masculine and feminine singular forms.
diagnosis: Student missed the gender distinction in the את-suffix forms.
remediation_hint: Compare the final letters and re-label the form for masculine or feminine singular.

observed_wrong_answer: Student confuses אתכם and אתכן.
expected_answer: Student distinguishes masculine plural and feminine plural forms.
diagnosis: Student missed the gender distinction inside the plural את-suffix forms.
remediation_hint: Use the ending to decide whether the form points to a masculine plural group or a feminine plural group.

observed_wrong_answer: Student confuses a singular possessive noun with a plural possessive noun.
expected_answer: Student parses the noun and its number correctly.
diagnosis: Student did not track the singular/plural difference after suffixes were added.
remediation_hint: Compare the whole suffixed noun carefully instead of focusing only on the final letters.

observed_wrong_answer: Student confuses my/your/his/her suffixes.
expected_answer: Student identifies the correct owner from the suffix family.
diagnosis: Student has not stabilized the possessive suffix table for singular nouns.
remediation_hint: Review the possessive suffix chart and match each ending to its owner label.

observed_wrong_answer: Student misses the plural י in a plural possessive form.
expected_answer: Student notices the added י before the suffix.
diagnosis: Student missed a key visual clue for plural possessive structure.
remediation_hint: Look one letter before the ending and check whether an added י is helping signal the plural possessive form.

observed_wrong_answer: Student translates מים or פנים as ordinary plural English words every time.
expected_answer: Student preserves the caution that these forms can translate as singular ideas in simple contexts.
diagnosis: Student overgeneralized the surface plural ending into an English plural meaning.
remediation_hint: Treat these as lesson-level caution words and do not force an ordinary English plural automatically.

observed_wrong_answer: Student treats a verb like a noun or a noun like a verb.
expected_answer: Student classifies the word before applying agreement or suffix rules.
diagnosis: Student confused noun and verb roles during analysis.
remediation_hint: Identify whether the word is naming something or showing an action before you apply later grammar rules.

observed_wrong_answer: Student gives a root meaning but misses the tense marker.
expected_answer: Student identifies the verb tense as part of the final meaning.
diagnosis: Student noticed the shoresh but missed the tense clue.
remediation_hint: After finding the shoresh, look again for added letters that show when the action happens.

observed_wrong_answer: Student confuses a past form with a future form.
expected_answer: Student distinguishes past endings from future markers.
diagnosis: Student mixed up the workbook's past and future paradigms.
remediation_hint: Compare where the grammar clue appears: many past clues sit at the end, while many future clues begin the verb.

observed_wrong_answer: Student confuses I / you / he / she / we / they.
expected_answer: Student labels person correctly before translating.
diagnosis: Student did not stabilize person labels in the parse.
remediation_hint: Label the form for person first, then refine number and gender before translating.

observed_wrong_answer: Student misses a masculine/feminine distinction.
expected_answer: Student tracks gender accurately in noun or suffix analysis.
diagnosis: Student is not yet using gender as an active grammar clue.
remediation_hint: Re-check whether the noun, suffix, or verb form is marking masculine or feminine.

observed_wrong_answer: Student misses a singular/plural distinction.
expected_answer: Student identifies whether the form is singular or plural.
diagnosis: Student is not yet reading number consistently in nouns or related grammar markers.
remediation_hint: Look for number clues in endings, suffixes, and agreement before choosing the translation.

observed_wrong_answer: Student translates Hebrew word order too literally into unnatural English.
expected_answer: Student reorders the English naturally while preserving the Hebrew meaning.
diagnosis: Student copied Hebrew order mechanically instead of translating the clause naturally.
remediation_hint: Identify the Hebrew parts first, then say the meaning in natural English order.
